results.title: Chained signature with checkpoint usage disabled can bypass all checkpointer validation
results.reasoning: No finding in the audit matches the specific bug where a chained signature with the checkpointer-usage flag (0x40) unset causes recover/recoverChained to proceed with _checkpointer=0 and _ignoreCheckpointer=true, thereby bypassing all checkpointer validation. The closest is Finding #24, which concerns the snapshot checkpoint floor being bypassed after an imageHash match in recoverChained, but that assumes a snapshot is provided and matched and proposes a different fix (enforcing prevCheckpoint >= snapshot.checkpoint). Fixing #24 would not address the unchecked-flag chained-signature bypass, and requiring the flag for chained signatures (the proposed fix for the described issue) would not fix #24. Therefore, the reported vulnerability is not detected in the audit content.

results.title: Partial signature replay/frontrunning attack on session calls
results.reasoning: The report does not identify the partial signature replay/frontrunning issue where per‑call session signatures can be reused after a BEHAVIOR_REVERT_ON_ERROR revert because the nonce consumption is reverted, allowing subset execution of previously signed calls. While finding #26 discusses session signature replay, it is a different vector (cross‑wallet replay due to missing wallet binding in hashCallWithReplayProtection) and proposes a different fix (bind to wallet). It does not mention Calls.execute/_execute revert behavior, nonce rollbacks, or binding signatures to the full payload to prevent partial subset replay. Fixing #26 would not fix the described partial replay/frontrun attack, so the specific vulnerability was not detected.
